    <h1 {...{
      "id": "add-modal-dialogs"
    }}>{`Add Modal Dialogs`}</h1>
    <p>{`Learn how to create a Modal Dialog as a user interface for Commands or as an additional UI for Panels`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "overview"
    }}>{`Overview`}</h2>
    <p>{`Modal dialogs are temporary windows that appear on top of the main application interface, demanding user attention before they can continue working. Unlike panels, which can be docked alongside Premiere's workspace, `}<strong parentName="p">{`modal dialogs block interaction with the host application until dismissed`}</strong>{`—making them ideal for critical decisions, input forms, or alerts that require immediate user response.`}</p>
    <p>{`In UXP, modal dialogs serve two primary purposes:`}</p>
    <ol>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Command UIs`}</strong>{`: Provide an interface for command entrypoints that don't need a persistent panel but require user input or display information.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Panel dialogs`}</strong>{`: Launch additional interfaces from panels to gather input, show settings, or display detailed information without cluttering the main panel.`}</li>
    </ol>
    <p>{`You'll learn how to:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Create modal dialogs triggered by command entrypoints`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Display modal dialogs from panel entrypoints`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Control dialog size and behavior`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Handle user input and dialog results`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "key-concepts"
    }}>{`Key Concepts`}</h2>
    <p>{`Modal dialogs share the same HTML document as your plugin, similar to `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../add-panels/index.md"
      }}>{`multiple panels`}</a>{`, but they `}<strong parentName="p">{`appear as floating windows`}</strong>{` that must be closed before the user can interact with Premiere again. This makes them perfect for workflows that require focused user attention—like configuration wizards, confirmation prompts, or data entry forms.`}</p>
    <p>{`In their simplest form, modal dialogs are just a `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`<dialog>`}</inlineCode>{` element in the HTML document launched with a `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`uxpShowModal()`}</inlineCode>{` method.`}</p>

    <h4 {...{

    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-html"
      }}>{`<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
  <script src="main.js"></script>
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="style.css" />
</head>
<body>
  <!-- Panel content -->
  <sp-heading>Open Modal Dialog</sp-heading>
  <sp-button id="openDialogBtn">Click</sp-button>

  <!-- Modal dialog content (hidden by default) -->
  <dialog>
    <sp-heading>👋 Hello Modal Dialog!</sp-heading>
    <sp-divider size="L"></sp-divider>
    <sp-body>Modal body content</sp-body>
  </dialog>

</body>
</html>
`}</code></pre>

    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-javascript"
      }}>{`const openDialogBtn = document.getElementById("openDialogBtn");
openDialogBtn.addEventListener("click", () => {
  const dialog = document.querySelector("dialog");
  dialog.uxpShowModal({                      // 👈
    title: "Demo Modal Dialog",              // 👈
    resize: "none",                          // 👈
    size: { width: 300, height: 300 },       // 👈
  });                                        // 👈
});
`}</code></pre>

  <sp-heading>Open Modal Dialog</sp-heading>

    <sp-heading>👋 Hello Another Modal Dialog!</sp-heading>

  </dialog>

});
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`Additionally, you can `}<strong parentName="p">{`chain modal dialogs`}</strong>{` by opening a modal dialog from another modal dialog. Just add the relevant UI elements and event handlers to the modal dialog you want to open from. Being chained, both dialogs are open at the same time, but the first one will be blocked until the second is closed.`}</p>

    <h2 {...{
      "id": "dialog-lifecycle-and-event-handling"
    }}>{`Dialog Lifecycle and Event Handling`}</h2>
    <p>{`Modal dialogs can be programmatically closed by calling the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`close()`}</inlineCode>{` method on the dialog element itself. This method takes an optional value that is returned to the code that called `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`uxpShowModal()`}</inlineCode>{` when the Promise resolves. You can use this value to pass data back to the caller or to signal how the dialog was dismissed.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "example-close-a-modal-dialog-and-return-a-value"
    }}>{`Example: Close a Modal Dialog and return a value`}</h3>
    <p>{`In this example, we'll create a modal dialog with two buttons: `}<strong parentName="p">{`OK`}</strong>{` and `}<strong parentName="p">{`Cancel`}</strong>{`. When the user clicks either button, the dialog closes and returns the corresponding value—`}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`"ok"`}</inlineCode>{` or `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`"cancel"`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</p>

            "srcSet": ["/uxp-premiere-pro/static/57d5e9c039cfd4da2b532ab021f685c5/dd4a7/add-modal-dialogs--close-event.png 320w", "/uxp-premiere-pro/static/57d5e9c039cfd4da2b532ab021f685c5/0f09e/add-modal-dialogs--close-event.png 640w", "/uxp-premiere-pro/static/57d5e9c039cfd4da2b532ab021f685c5/bbbf7/add-modal-dialogs--close-event.png 1280w", "/uxp-premiere-pro/static/57d5e9c039cfd4da2b532ab021f685c5/ac7a9/add-modal-dialogs--close-event.png 1920w", "/uxp-premiere-pro/static/57d5e9c039cfd4da2b532ab021f685c5/8c468/add-modal-dialogs--close-event.png 2133w"],
            "sizes": "(max-width: 1280px) 100vw, 1280px",
            "type": "image/png"
          }}></source>{`
          `}<img parentName="picture" {...{
            "className": "gatsby-resp-image-image",

            "alt": "Modal Dialog - close event",

    <p>{`Please note that, in order to capture the returned value, you need to use the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`await`}</inlineCode>{` keyword when calling `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`uxpShowModal()`}</inlineCode>{` and the "click" event handler on the buttons must be `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`async`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</p>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="2" languages="HTML, JavaScript" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "indexhtml-2"
    }}>{`index.html`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-html"
      }}>{`<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
  <script src="main.js"></script>
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="style.css" />
</head>

<body>
  <sp-heading>Open Modal Dialog</sp-heading>
  <sp-button id="openDialogBtn">Click</sp-button>

  <dialog>
    <sp-heading>👋 Hello Modal Dialog!</sp-heading>
    <sp-divider size="L"></sp-divider>
    <sp-body>Modal body content</sp-body>
    <sp-button-group>
      <sp-button id="closeDialogBtn">OK</sp-button>
      <sp-button id="cancelDialogBtn">Cancel</sp-button>
    </sp-button-group>
  </dialog>

</body>
</html>
`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "mainjs-2"
    }}>{`main.js`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-javascript"
      }}>{`// Modal Dialog reference
const mDialog = document.querySelector("dialog");

// Modal dialog buttons event handlers -----------------------
mDialog?.querySelector("#closeDialogBtn")
  ?.addEventListener("click", (evt) => {
    mDialog.close("ok");
  });
// Cancel button event handler on the modal dialog
mDialog?.querySelector("#cancelDialogBtn")
  ?.addEventListener("click", (evt) => {
    mDialog.close("cancel");
  });

// Open dialog button event handler -------------------------
document
  .querySelector("#openDialogBtn")
  ?.addEventListener("click", async () => {
    console.log("Opening dialog");
    const res = await mDialog?.uxpShowModal({
      title: "Demo Modal Dialog",
      resize: "none",
      size: { width: 300, height: 300 },
    });
    console.log("Dialog closed with:", res); // 👈 "ok", "cancel", or
                                             // 👈 "reasonCanceled"!
  });
`}</code></pre>
    <InlineAlert variant="warning" slots="header, text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`Dialog dismissal`}</p>
    <p>{`When users close the dialog by clicking the `}<strong parentName="p">{`red dot`}</strong>{` (Mac) or `}<strong parentName="p">{`X`}</strong>{` button (Windows) in the title bar, or by pressing the `}<strong parentName="p">{`Esc`}</strong>{` key, the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`close()`}</inlineCode>{` method is called with the value `}<strong parentName="p"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`"reasonCanceled"`}</inlineCode></strong>{`. Make sure to handle this value in your code to provide a consistent user experience.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "modal-dialogs-as-command-uis"
    }}>{`Modal Dialogs as Command UIs`}</h2>
    <p>{`Modal dialogs are perfect to show a simple interface for a command, when the built-in `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../../uxp-api/reference-js/Global%20Members/HTML%20DOM/alert.md"
      }}><inlineCode parentName="a">{`alert()`}</inlineCode></a>{`, `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../../uxp-api/reference-js/Global%20Members/HTML%20DOM/prompt.md"
      }}><inlineCode parentName="a">{`prompt()`}</inlineCode></a>{` and `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../../uxp-api/reference-js/Global%20Members/HTML%20DOM/confirm.md"
      }}><inlineCode parentName="a">{`confirm()`}</inlineCode></a>{` methods are not enough. The example below shows how to create a dialog for an About command.`}</p>

    <p>{`These are often very simple UIs, used to show information about the plugin or copyright data.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "example-about-dialog"
    }}>{`Example: About dialog`}</h3>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="2" languages="JavaScript, json" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "mainjs-3"
    }}>{`main.js`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-javascript"
      }}>{`const { entrypoints, host, versions } = require("uxp");
const manifest = require("./manifest.json");
const os = require("os");

entrypoints.setup({
  commands: {
    "about-command": async () => {
      // Create the dialog dynamically (or load from an HTML file)
      const dialog = document.createElement("dialog");
      dialog.innerHTML = \`
        <sp-heading>Clip Mixer</sp-heading>
        <sp-divider size="L"></sp-divider>
        <sp-body>🙌 Thanks for using Clip Mixer v\${manifest.version}!</sp-body>
        <sp-body><b>Application:</b> \${host.name} v\${
        host.version
      } (\${os.platform()})</sp-body>
        <sp-body><b>UXP Runtime:</b> \${versions.uxp} - <b>Plugin Version:</b> \${
        versions.plugin
      }</sp-body>
      \`.trim();
      // ☝ trim is a safety measure to avoid whitespace issues

      document.body.appendChild(dialog);

      // Add styles programmatically using element.style
      dialog.style.color = "white";
      dialog.style.padding = "16px";
      dialog.querySelector("sp-divider").style.margin = "0 0 16px 0";
      dialog.querySelector("sp-heading").style.margin = "0 0 16px 0";

      // Show modal
      await dialog.uxpShowModal({
        title: "Command Modal Dialog",
        resize: "none",
        size: { width: 300, height: 200 },
      });
    },
  },
});
`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "manifestjson"
    }}>{`manifest.json`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-json"
      }}>{`{
  "id": "Test-modaldialog",
  "name": "Test-modaldialog",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "main": "main.js",
  "host": { "app": "premierepro", "minVersion": "25.6.0" },
  "manifestVersion": 5,
  "entrypoints": [
    {
      "id": "about-command",
      "type": "command",
      "label": "About..."
    }
  ],
  "icons": [ /* ... icons ... */ ],
}
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`In the example above, the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`about-command`}</inlineCode>{` is defined in the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`entrypoints`}</inlineCode>{` array of the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`manifest.json`}</inlineCode>{` file and implemented via `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`entrypoints.setup()`}</inlineCode>{` in the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`main.js`}</inlineCode>{` file—see `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../concepts/panels-and-commands/index.md#commands"
      }}>{`this guide`}</a>{` if you need a refresher on Commands concepts, and `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../add-commands/index.md"
      }}>{`this tutorial`}</a>{` for implementation examples.`}</p>
    <p>{`Also note that the entire UI is created with a mix of JavaScript methods (for the parent `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`<dialog>`}</inlineCode>{` element and the styling), and Template Literals for easier readability.`}</p>
    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="header, text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`Trim Template Literals`}</p>
    <p>{`When using Template Literals for the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`dialog.innerHTML`}</inlineCode>{` property, it's a good practice to use the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`trim()`}</inlineCode>{` method to remove any whitespace from the beginning and end of the string, which would otherwise create problems.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "load-external-html-files"
    }}>{`Load external HTML files`}</h2>
    <p>{`To keep your code organized, you can load the HTML content from an external file. In UXP, it is extremely easy to do so using the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`fetch()`}</inlineCode>{` method; just make sure to handle the response as a text string and remember that the methods are asynchronous. Using the same structure from the previous example, we can load the HTML content from an external file and use it to fill the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`dialog.innerHTML`}</inlineCode>{` property.`}</p>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="3" languages="JavaScript, html, json" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "mainjs-4"
    }}>{`main.js`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-javascript"
      }}>{`const { entrypoints, host, versions } = require("uxp");
const manifest = require("./manifest.json");
const os = require("os");

entrypoints.setup({
  commands: {
    "about-command": async () => {
      // Load the HTML content from an external file
      // In this case, the _dialog.html file is located in the
      // same directory as the main.js file
      const dialogHtml = await fetch("./_dialog.html") // 👈
        .then((res) => res.text())    // 👈 handle the response as text
      console.log("About command");
      const dialog = document.createElement("dialog");
      dialog.innerHTML = dialogHtml.trim();
      // ...
      // Replace the placeholders with the actual values. It is the
      // equivalent of using Template Literals in the previous example
      dialog.querySelector("#version").textContent = manifest.version;
      dialog.querySelector("#app-name").textContent = host.name;
      dialog.querySelector("#app-version").textContent = host.version;
      dialog.querySelector("#platform").textContent = os.platform();
      dialog.querySelector("#uxp-version").textContent = versions.uxp;
      dialog.querySelector("#plugin-version").textContent = versions.plugin;
      // ...
      document.body.appendChild(dialog);
      // ...
      const result = await dialog.uxpShowModal({ /* ... */ });
    }
  }
});
`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "_dialoghtml"
    }}>{`_`}{`dialog.html`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-html"
      }}>{`<sp-heading>Clip Mixer</sp-heading>
<sp-divider size="L"></sp-divider>
<sp-body>🙌 Thanks for using Clip Mixer v<span id="version"></span>!</sp-body>
<sp-body><b>Application:</b> <span id="app-name"></span> v<span id="app-version"></span> (<span id="platform"></span>)</sp-body>
<sp-body><b>UXP Runtime:</b> <span id="uxp-version"></span> - <b>Plugin Version:</b> <span id="plugin-version"></span></sp-body>
`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "manifestjson-1"
    }}>{`manifest.json`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-json"
      }}>{`{
  "id": "Test-modaldialog",
  "name": "Test-modaldialog",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "main": "main.js",
  "host": { "app": "premierepro", "minVersion": "25.6.0" },
  "manifestVersion": 5,
  "entrypoints": [
    {
      "id": "about-command",
      "type": "command",
      "label": "About..."
    }
  ],
  "icons": [ /* ... icons ... */ ],
}
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`Compared to the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "#example-about-dialog"
      }}>{`previous example`}</a>{`, in this one it is slightly more tedious to fill the dialog with values: with Template Literals you can use the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`\${}`}</inlineCode>{` syntax to insert the values directly into the string, but with external HTML files you need to use the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`querySelector()`}</inlineCode>{` method to select the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`<span>`}</inlineCode>{` elements and set the values programmatically.`}</p>
    <p>{`Please note that the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`"main"`}</inlineCode>{` property in the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`manifest.json`}</inlineCode>{` file is still set to `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`"main.js"`}</inlineCode>{`, a JavaScript file—even if we are loading the dialog content from an external `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`.html`}</inlineCode>{` file.`}</p>
    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="header, text, text2" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`Use `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`fetch()`}</inlineCode>{` and `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`require()`}</inlineCode>{` with external files`}</p>
    <p>{`In the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "#example-about-dialog"
      }}>{`original About dialog example`}</a>{`, we used `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`require()`}</inlineCode>{` to import the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`manifest.json`}</inlineCode>{` file and extract the plugin version. This is a handy trick to access the plugin's metadata from the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`main.js`}</inlineCode>{` file; in general, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`require()`}</inlineCode>{` works for any `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`.js`}</inlineCode>{` and `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`.json`}</inlineCode>{` files that are local to the plugin.`}</p>
    <p>{`For all other file formats, you may use the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`fetch()`}</inlineCode>{` or the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`fs`}</inlineCode>{` methods illustrated in the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../../resources/recipes/filesystem-operations/index.md#the-fs-module"
      }}>{`Working with the file system`}</a>{` tutorial.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "singleton-pattern-for-modal-dialogs"
    }}>{`Singleton pattern for Modal Dialogs`}</h2>
    <p>{`For complex dialogs, we recommend using a Singleton pattern to encapsulate the dialog logic and UI. This approach lets you separate dialog creation, initialization, and running logic in a way that's both maintainable and safe against handler duplication when your script is re-evaluated.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-javascript"
      }}>{`class ModalDialog {
  // Singleton support
  static #instance;

  // Private state
  #dialog;
  #params; // (optional) store validated values collected from the UI

  // Enforce the singleton: return the existing instance if present
  constructor() {
    if (ModalDialog.#instance) return ModalDialog.#instance;
    ModalDialog.#instance = this;
  }
  // get (or lazily create) the singleton instance
  static getInstance() {
    if (!ModalDialog.#instance) ModalDialog.#instance = new ModalDialog();
    return ModalDialog.#instance;
  }

  // Build the dialog UI and assign to #dialog
  async createDialog() { /* ... */ }

  // Set defaults and wire listeners
  initDialog() { /* ... */ }

  // Show the dialog and return a Promise with the result
  async runDialog() { /* ... */ }

  // Private: execute Host App DOM logic as needed
  async #runRoutine() { /* ... */ }
}

// Example usage
try {
  const modalDialog = ModalDialog.getInstance();
  await modalDialog.createDialog();
  modalDialog.initDialog();
  const res = await modalDialog.runDialog();
  res;
} catch (error) {
  console.error("Argh!", error);
}
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`A brief explanation of the properties and methods is as follows.`}</p>
    <table>
      <thead parentName="table">
        <tr parentName="thead">
          <th parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}>{`Property/Method`}</th>
          <th parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}>{`Description`}</th>
        </tr>
      </thead>
      <tbody parentName="table">
        <tr parentName="tbody">
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}><inlineCode parentName="td">{`static #instance`}</inlineCode></td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}>{`Private static field holding the `}<strong parentName="td">{`singleton instance`}</strong>{`. Ensures only one dialog object exists and is reused.`}</td>
        </tr>
        <tr parentName="tbody">
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}><inlineCode parentName="td">{`#dialog`}</inlineCode></td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}>{`Private `}<strong parentName="td">{`reference to the`}</strong>{` `}<inlineCode parentName="td">{`<dialog>`}</inlineCode>{` element; used internally to show/close the modal.`}</td>
        </tr>
        <tr parentName="tbody">
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}><inlineCode parentName="td">{`#params`}</inlineCode></td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}>{`Private `}<strong parentName="td">{`state container for validated values`}</strong>{` gathered from the UI (optional but useful for passing data to routines).`}</td>
        </tr>
        <tr parentName="tbody">
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}><inlineCode parentName="td">{`constructor()`}</inlineCode></td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}><strong parentName="td">{`Enforces the singleton`}</strong>{` by returning the existing instance when one already exists.`}</td>
        </tr>
        <tr parentName="tbody">
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}><inlineCode parentName="td">{`static getInstance()`}</inlineCode></td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}><strong parentName="td">{`Convenience accessor`}</strong>{` that returns (or lazily creates) the singleton instance. Prefer this over `}<inlineCode parentName="td">{`new`}</inlineCode>{` in calling code.`}</td>
        </tr>
        <tr parentName="tbody">
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}><inlineCode parentName="td">{`async createDialog()`}</inlineCode></td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}><strong parentName="td">{`Builds the dialog UI`}</strong>{` and assigns it to `}<inlineCode parentName="td">{`#dialog`}</inlineCode>{`. Designed to be called once and then reused on subsequent opens.`}</td>
        </tr>
        <tr parentName="tbody">
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}><inlineCode parentName="td">{`initDialog()`}</inlineCode></td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}><strong parentName="td">{`Initializes`}</strong>{` the dialog with default values and `}<strong parentName="td">{`attaching event listeners`}</strong>{` (e.g., button clicks, form changes, focus/blur events, etc.)`}</td>
        </tr>
        <tr parentName="tbody">
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}><inlineCode parentName="td">{`async runDialog()`}</inlineCode></td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}><strong parentName="td">{`Displays the modal`}</strong>{` and resolves when the dialog closes.`}</td>
        </tr>
        <tr parentName="tbody">
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}><inlineCode parentName="td">{`async #runRoutine()`}</inlineCode></td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}>{`Private method to `}<strong parentName="td">{`run any Host App DOM code`}</strong>{` using the data collected in the dialog.`}</td>
        </tr>
      </tbody>
    </table>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "example-real-world-dialog"
    }}>{`Example: real-world Dialog`}</h3>
    <p>{`Let's see a practical example of how to use the Singleton pattern to create a modal dialog opened by a Panel. The files are organized as follows:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-text"
      }}>{` 📁 .                    Plugin root directory
 ├── 📂 fragments        Dialog-specific files
 │   ├── 📄 dialog.html  Modal dialog UI markup
 │   └── 🎨 styles.css   Dialog-specific styles
 ├── 📄 index.html       Main panel UI
 ├── 🎨 style.css        Main panel styles
 ├── ⚙️ main.js          Main panel and Dialog logic
 └── 📋 manifest.json    Plugin configuration
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`From the files tree alone, you can see that we'll make use of `}<strong parentName="p">{`fragments`}</strong>{` to keep the dialog UI and styles separate and avoid using Template Literals or, worse, create all the elements programmatically in the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`main.js`}</inlineCode>{` file.`}</p>

    <p>{`The dialog we'll create will be a fictitious "Set sequence dimensions" tool, that allows the user to set the width and height of the sequence. It's stripped from Premiere's API code, but we've kept the logic and the UI as close as possible to the real thing.`}</p>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="6" languages="HTML, CSS, JavaScript, HTML, CSS, JSON" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "indexhtml-3"
    }}>{`index.html`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-html"
      }}>{`<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>

  <link rel="stylesheet" href="style.css" />
</head>

<body>
  <sp-heading>Open Modal Dialog</sp-heading>
  <sp-button id="openDialogBtn">Open Dialog (singleton)</sp-button>
</body>
</html>
`}</code></pre>

sp-divider, sp-heading { margin: 0 0 16px 0; }

    <h4 {...{
      "id": "mainjs-5"
    }}>{`main.js`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-javascript"
      }}>{`// Global constants for the dialog
const G = {
  version: "1.0.0",
  title: "Input required...",
  dialogSize: { width: 240, height: 150 },
  defaultValues: { width: 1920, height: 1080 },
  valueRange: { min: 320, max: 10240 },
};

class ModalDialog {
  static #instance; // Singleton instance
  #dialog;          // Reference to the dialog element
  // State container for validated values
  #params = { width: 0, height: 0 };

  constructor() {
    // Enforce singleton pattern
    if (ModalDialog.#instance) { return ModalDialog.#instance; }
    ModalDialog.#instance = this;
  }

  // Static getter for singleton instance (optional, cleaner API)
  static getInstance() {
    if (!ModalDialog.#instance) {
      ModalDialog.#instance = new ModalDialog();
    }
    return ModalDialog.#instance;
  }

  // Create and populate a dialog w/ HTML elements
  // Assign to #dialog
  // Uses the same pattern as styles: create once, reuse thereafter
  async createDialog() {
    // Add scoped styles to the head
    // The conditional check ensures the styles are added only once
    // to avoid duplicates when the dialog is opened multiple times
    if (!document.querySelector("#modal-dialog-styles")) {
      const styleEl = document.createElement("style");
      styleEl.id = "modal-dialog-styles";
      styleEl.textContent = (
        await fetch("./fragments/styles.css").then((res) => res.text())
      ).trim();
      document.head.appendChild(styleEl);
    }

    // Same pattern for the dialog: create once, reuse thereafter
    if (!document.querySelector("#modal-dialog")) {
      this.#dialog = document.createElement("dialog");
      this.#dialog.id = "modal-dialog";
      // Add unique class for scoping the CSS
      this.#dialog.classList.add("modal-dialog");
      this.#dialog.innerHTML = (
        await fetch("./fragments/dialog.html").then((res) => res.text())
      ).trim();
      document.body.appendChild(this.#dialog);
    } else {
      // Reuse existing dialog
      this.#dialog = document.querySelector("#modal-dialog");
    }
  }

  // Populate w/ default values
  // Attach event listeners (only once)
  initDialog() {
    const wField = this.#dialog.querySelector("#width");
    const hField = this.#dialog.querySelector("#height");

    // Always reset to default values on each init
    wField.value = G.defaultValues.width.toString();
    hField.value = G.defaultValues.height.toString();

    // Attach event listeners only once; check the dialog element itself
    // since the dialog persists but new instances are created each time
    if (!this.#dialog.dataset.listenersAttached) {
      // Sanitize input for the textfields
      const sanitizeInput = (evt) => {
        /* ... */
      };
      wField.addEventListener("input", sanitizeInput);
      hField.addEventListener("input", sanitizeInput);

      // Validate and return params object if valid, null if invalid
      const validateAndGetParams = () => {
        const w = parseInt(wField.value);
        const h = parseInt(hField.value);
        if (
          isNaN(w) || isNaN(h) ||
          w < G.valueRange.min || w > G.valueRange.max ||
          h < G.valueRange.min || h > G.valueRange.max
        ) {
          return null;
        }
        return { width: w, height: h };
      };

      this.#dialog
        .querySelector("#okDialogBtn")
        .addEventListener("click", () => {
          const params = validateAndGetParams();
          if (params) {
            // Store validated params for use in #runRoutine
            this.#params = params;
            this.#dialog.close("ok");
          } else {
            // alert the user that the parameters are invalid
            alert("Invalid parameters");
          }
        });
      this.#dialog
        .querySelector("#cancelDialogBtn")
        .addEventListener("click", () => {
          this.#dialog.close("cancel");
        });

      this.#dialog.addEventListener("keydown", (evt) => {
        if (evt.key === "Enter") {
          const params = validateAndGetParams();
          if (params) {
            this.#params = params;
            this.#dialog.close("ok");
          }
        }
      });

      // Mark the dialog element itself as having listeners attached
      this.#dialog.dataset.listenersAttached = "true";
    }
  }

  async runDialog() {
    const rv = await this.#dialog.uxpShowModal({
      title: G.title,
      resize: "none",
      size: G.dialogSize,
    });

    if (rv === "ok") {
      console.log("Dialog closed with OK");
      const res = await this.#runRoutine();
      if (res === true) return true;
      throw res; // bubble any routine error
    }

    if (rv === "cancel" || rv === "reasonCanceled") {
      throw "cancel";
    }

    throw "Dialog closed unexpectedly";
  }

  // Run whatever Host App DOM code is needed
  // Has access to validated params via this.#params
  async #runRoutine() {
    console.log("Running PPro routine with params:", this.#params);
    //... perform the (fictitious) routine using
    // this.#params.width and this.#params.height
    return true;
  }

  // Getter to access validated params (if needed by external code)
  getParams() {
    // Return a copy to prevent external mutation
    return { ...this.#params };
  }
}

const btn = document.querySelector("#openDialogBtn");
if (btn && !btn.dataset.listenerAttached) {
  btn.addEventListener("click", async () => {
    try {
      // const modalDialog = new ModalDialog() // 👈 same 👇
      const modalDialog = ModalDialog.getInstance();
      await modalDialog.createDialog();
      modalDialog.initDialog();
      const res = await modalDialog.runDialog();
      res;
    } catch (error) {
      console.error("Arrgh!", error);
    }
  });
  // ✅ ensure we only wire the listener once
  btn.dataset.listenerAttached = "true";
}
`}</code></pre>

  </div>
  <sp-button-group>

        "className": "language-css"
      }}>{`/* Everything is scoped for the modal dialog only */
dialog.modal-dialog { padding: 0; }

dialog.modal-dialog .wrapper {
  min-height: 100vh; overflow: hidden;
  padding: 10px 20px; display: flex; flex-direction: column;
}

dialog.modal-dialog .row {
  display: flex; flex-direction: row; justify-content: space-between;
}

  display: flex; width: 92px; margin-bottom: 6px;
}

    }}>{`manifest.json`}</h4>

    <h3 {...{
      "id": "implementation-details"
    }}>{`Implementation Details`}</h3>
    <p>{`This example demonstrates a production-ready approach to modal dialogs, combining several best practices into a cohesive pattern. Let's examine each file and understand why the code is structured this way.`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "1-the-panel-ui-indexhtml-and-stylecss"
    }}>{`1. The Panel UI: `}<inlineCode parentName="h4">{`index.html`}</inlineCode>{` and `}<inlineCode parentName="h4">{`style.css`}</inlineCode></h4>
    <p>{`The main panel is intentionally minimal, just a heading and a button. Nothing different from what we've seen so far.`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "2-the-manifest-manifestjson"
    }}>{`2. The Manifest: `}<inlineCode parentName="h4">{`manifest.json`}</inlineCode></h4>
    <p>{`The manifest is standard for a panel entrypoint, with the notable inclusion of the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`enableAlerts`}</inlineCode>{` feature flag, `}<strong parentName="p">{`required to use the `}<inlineCode parentName="strong">{`alert()`}</inlineCode>{` method`}</strong>{` we call in `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`initDialog()`}</inlineCode>{` when validation fails.`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "3-the-dialog-ui-fragmentsdialoghtml-and-fragmentsstylescss"
    }}>{`3. The Dialog UI: `}<inlineCode parentName="h4">{`fragments/dialog.html`}</inlineCode>{` and `}<inlineCode parentName="h4">{`fragments/styles.css`}</inlineCode></h4>
    <p>{`The dialog markup lives in a separate `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`fragments/`}</inlineCode>{` directory—a pattern borrowed from web development that keeps the dialog UI portable and maintainable. Please note that the whole `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`dialog.html`}</inlineCode>{` content is wrapped with a `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`<div>`}</inlineCode>{` element, as the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`<dialog>`}</inlineCode>{` element itself will be created dynamically in the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`main.js`}</inlineCode>{` file.`}</p>
    <p>{`The styles use a `}<strong parentName="p">{`scoping technique`}</strong>{` to prevent conflicts with the panel styles; in fact, the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`<dialog>`}</inlineCode>{` element will added a `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`modal-dialog`}</inlineCode>{` class, so the styles will be scoped to the elements it contains and won't leak out. Without it, generic class names like `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`.wrapper`}</inlineCode>{` or `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`.row`}</inlineCode>{` would clash between your dialog and panel styles.`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "4-the-core-logic-mainjs"
    }}>{`4. The Core Logic: `}<inlineCode parentName="h4">{`main.js`}</inlineCode></h4>
    <p>{`This is where the real work happens. Let's break down the implementation piece by piece.`}</p>
    <h5 {...{
      "id": "global-configuration-object"
    }}>{`Global Configuration Object`}</h5>
    <p>{`The `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`G`}</inlineCode>{` object centralizes all configuration in one place, and contains title, size, default values, and validation ranges.`}</p>
    <h5 {...{
      "id": "singleton-pattern"
    }}>{`Singleton Pattern`}</h5>
    <p>{`The Singleton pattern ensures `}<strong parentName="p">{`only one instance of the dialog class exists throughout the plugin's lifetime`}</strong>{`. Without the singleton, you'd create duplicate dialog elements and duplicate event listeners every time the script runs—leading to memory leaks and unpredictable behavior.`}</p>
    <p>{`The `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`static #instance`}</inlineCode>{` field is `}<strong parentName="p">{`private to the class`}</strong>{` (note the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`#`}</inlineCode>{` prefix). When you call `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`new ModalDialog()`}</inlineCode>{`, the constructor checks if an instance already exists, and returns that instance instead of creating a new one. The `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`getInstance()`}</inlineCode>{` static method provides a cleaner API—it's the `}<strong parentName="p">{`preferred way to get the singleton`}</strong>{` and makes the intent explicit.`}</p>
    <p>{`The `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`#dialog`}</inlineCode>{` field stores a reference to the actual `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`<dialog>`}</inlineCode>{` DOM element, while `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`#params`}</inlineCode>{` holds the validated user input (width and height values in our case) once the user clicks OK. Both are private fields that don't need to be accessed externally.`}</p>
    <h5 {...{
      "id": "createdialog"
    }}><inlineCode parentName="h5">{`createDialog()`}</inlineCode></h5>
    <p>{`This method does the heavy lifting of constructing the dialog UI; it's `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`async`}</inlineCode>{` because it uses `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`fetch()`}</inlineCode>{` to load external files. Notice that before creating anything it checks if the element already exists in the DOM using `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`document.querySelector()`}</inlineCode>{`. This is critical for performance and correctness—`}<strong parentName="p">{`you don't want multiple copies of the same dialog`}</strong>{` cluttering the DOM.`}</p>
    <p>{`The method injects the CSS into a `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`<style>`}</inlineCode>{` element in the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`<head>`}</inlineCode>{`. It assigns an `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`id`}</inlineCode>{` to the style element (`}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`#modal-dialog-styles`}</inlineCode>{`) so subsequent calls can detect it and skip re-injection. The dialog element itself gets both an `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`id`}</inlineCode>{` (`}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`modal-dialog`}</inlineCode>{`) and a `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`class`}</inlineCode>{` (`}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`modal-dialog`}</inlineCode>{`). The ID is for `}<strong parentName="p">{`unique identification and DOM queries`}</strong>{`, while the class is for `}<strong parentName="p">{`CSS scoping`}</strong>{`—remember those `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`dialog.modal-dialog`}</inlineCode>{` selectors in `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`styles.css`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</p>
    <p>{`The `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`else`}</inlineCode>{` branch handles the case where the dialog already exists (because the singleton was reused). It simply `}<strong parentName="p">{`grabs the existing reference`}</strong>{` instead of creating a new one.`}</p>
    <h5 {...{
      "id": "initdialog"
    }}><inlineCode parentName="h5">{`initDialog()`}</inlineCode></h5>
    <p>{`Once the dialog exists in the DOM, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`initDialog()`}</inlineCode>{` `}<strong parentName="p">{`sets default values and wires up event listeners`}</strong>{`. This method splits into two logical sections:`}</p>
    <ol>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`State reset`}</strong>{`.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Event listener attachment`}</strong>{`.`}</li>
    </ol>
    <p>{`The first part `}<strong parentName="p">{`always runs`}</strong>{` when you call `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`initDialog()`}</inlineCode>{`—it resets the input fields to their default values. This ensures a clean slate every time the dialog opens. The second part—the event listener setup—is `}<strong parentName="p">{`guarded by a conditional check`}</strong>{`. It leverages HTML5 `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`data-*`}</inlineCode>{` attributes to track whether listeners have been attached. If they haven't, the method attaches them and sets the flag to `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`"true"`}</inlineCode>{`. This `}<strong parentName="p">{`prevents listener duplication`}</strong>{` when the dialog is opened multiple times—a common source of bugs.`}</p>
    <p>{`Several helper functions and event handlers are defined:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`sanitizeInput()`}</inlineCode></strong>{`: This placeholder function would normally filter out invalid characters or format the input as the user types.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`validateAndGetParams()`}</inlineCode></strong>{`: This function `}<strong parentName="li">{`validates the user's input`}</strong>{` and returns either an object with validated values or `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`null`}</inlineCode>{` if validation fails. It converts the string values to integers, checks for `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`NaN`}</inlineCode>{` (which happens if the user enters non-numeric text).`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`OK button handler`}</strong>{`: When clicked, it calls `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`validateAndGetParams()`}</inlineCode>{`. If validation succeeds, it `}<strong parentName="li">{`stores the validated parameters`}</strong>{` in `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`this.#params`}</inlineCode>{` (so they're available to other methods) and closes the dialog with the value `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`"ok"`}</inlineCode>{`. If validation fails, it shows an alert to inform the user. `}<strong parentName="li">{`Never silently fail validation`}</strong>{`, as it creates confusion.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Cancel button handler`}</strong>{`: Simply closes the dialog with the value `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`"cancel"`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Enter key handler`}</strong>{`: Provides `}<strong parentName="li">{`keyboard accessibility`}</strong>{` by allowing users to press Enter to submit the dialog instead of clicking OK.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`Note that the Enter key handler `}<strong parentName="p">{`doesn't close the dialog on invalid input`}</strong>{`. This is intentional: pressing Enter shouldn't do anything if the input is invalid, forcing the user to either correct the input or explicitly cancel. This prevents accidental submissions.`}</p>
    <h5 {...{
      "id": "rundialog"
    }}><inlineCode parentName="h5">{`runDialog()`}</inlineCode></h5>
    <p>{`This method `}<strong parentName="p">{`displays the modal and handles its result`}</strong>{`. It awaits `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`uxpShowModal()`}</inlineCode>{`, which `}<strong parentName="p">{`blocks execution until the dialog is dismissed`}</strong>{`. The return value (`}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`rv`}</inlineCode>{`) is whatever was passed to `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`dialog.close()`}</inlineCode>{` in the event handlers—either `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`"ok"`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`"cancel"`}</inlineCode>{`, or `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`"reasonCanceled"`}</inlineCode>{` (when the user closes the dialog via the title bar or Esc key).`}</p>
    <p>{`The method uses `}<strong parentName="p">{`exceptions for control flow`}</strong>{`: if the user cancels, it throws `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`"cancel"`}</inlineCode>{` rather than returning a value. This forces the calling code to handle cancellation explicitly in a `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`catch`}</inlineCode>{` block, which is a common pattern for operations that can be aborted. If the dialog closes with OK, it calls the private `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`#runRoutine()`}</inlineCode>{` method to perform the actual work (Premiere API calls, in our case).`}</p>
    <h5 {...{
      "id": "runroutine"

    <p>{`This private method is where you'd perform `}<strong parentName="p">{`any Host Application DOM operations`}</strong>{` using the collected parameters. The `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`#runRoutine()`}</inlineCode>{` call is also error-aware: if it returns `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`true`}</inlineCode>{`, everything succeeded. If it returns anything else (presumably an error message or error object), `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`runDialog()`}</inlineCode>{` `}<strong parentName="p">{`re-throws that error`}</strong>{` to bubble it up to the caller. This layered error handling ensures that failures at any level—validation, user cancellation, or routine execution—are properly communicated.`}</p>

    <h2 {...{
      "id": "summary"
    }}>{`Summary`}</h2>
    <p>{`Modal dialogs are temporary, blocking windows that demand user attention before they can continue working with Premiere—ideal for critical decisions, input forms, or alerts.`}</p>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Key Concepts:`}</strong></p>
    <ol>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Basic structure`}</strong>{`: Modal dialogs use a `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`<dialog>`}</inlineCode>{` HTML element launched with the `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`uxpShowModal()`}</inlineCode>{` method, which returns a Promise that resolves when the dialog is closed.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Two primary use cases`}</strong>{`:`}
        <ul parentName="li">
          <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Command UIs`}</strong>{`: Provide interfaces for command entrypoints without needing persistent panels.`}</li>
          <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Panel dialogs`}</strong>{`: Launch additional interfaces from panels to gather input or display information.`}</li>
        </ul>
      </li>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Multiple and chained dialogs`}</strong>{`: One panel can launch multiple independent modal dialogs (only one open at a time), and dialogs can be chained by opening one from another.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Dialog lifecycle`}</strong>{`: Use `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`dialog.close(value)`}</inlineCode>{` to dismiss dialogs and return values to the calling code. Handle the `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`"reasonCanceled"`}</inlineCode>{` return value when users close via the title bar or Esc key.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Best practices`}</strong>{`: For complex dialogs, use the Singleton pattern to prevent duplicate DOM elements and event listeners. Load external HTML files with `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`fetch()`}</inlineCode>{` and scope CSS to prevent style conflicts.`}</li>
    </ol>
